mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding labor income and aggregate demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of PAYEMS lagged change versus 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the top PAYEMS-growth quintile fails to outperform the bottom quintile by more than 2% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.5 percentage points over a three-month window signals a deteriorating labor market that erodes consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In walk-forward tests, SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where the trigger fires, or the triggered signal Rank IC is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrently rising FEDFUNDS and rising CPIAUCSL indicate a stagflationary monetary squeeze where the central bank is tightening into an inflationary supply shock, jointly raising real discount rates and input costs, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (FEDFUNDS change × CPIAUCSL change) produces a 20-day walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year window, or the joint signal underperforms the standalone FEDFUNDS signal in a Granger sense.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions characterized by low unemployment and elevated policy rates, a month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals an impending supply-side profit squeeze and inventory correction that leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When the regime trigger is active, SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 45% of INDPRO-decline instances, or the INDPRO change Rank IC is not negative (p > 0.05) conditional on the regime.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 2 percent signals a collapse in consumer spending that directly reduces corporate revenue expectations and triggers de-risking, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward triggered instances show positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 55% over any 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP (GDPC1) captures aggregate demand expansion and capacity utilization improvements that lift corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The top quintile of GDPC1 growth realizations does not outperform the bottom quintile by at least 3% annualized over any 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously decelerating nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and a rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicate a broad-based labor market deterioration that is more severe than either signal alone, eroding consumer income and spending faster than consensus expects, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (PAYEMS change × UNRATE change) produces a 20-day walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year window, or the joint signal fails to outperform either standalone signal in predicting negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated federal funds rate above 5 percent signals a restrictive monetary policy stance that raises the risk-free discount rate and debt-service costs, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward instances with FEDFUNDS > 5% show positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases, or the high-rate regime underperforms the low-rate regime in a t-test (p > 0.05).
